file_insights.changes: Added RetryTransient command variant to Command enum with optional id parameter and --all flag; added handler routing to cards::cmd_retry_transient
file_insights.pattern_consistency: Follows existing command structure (similar to Retry variant); proper doc comments and clap arg attributes
file_insights.integration_points: Wires into main match statement; passes id as Option<&str> and all as bool

file_insights.changes: Added stub function cmd_retry_transient with parameter validation logic returning placeholder anyhow::bail errors
file_insights.pattern_consistency: Error handling pattern matches cmd_retry and other card commands; underscore prefix on unused _root parameter
file_insights.integration_points: Called directly from main.rs; provides clear error messages for unimplemented cases

patterns_discovered: CLI command variant pattern: optional positional parameter (id: Option<String>) combined with optional flag (--all: bool)
Stub implementation pattern: placeholder errors guide future implementers while maintaining compile-time safety
Parameter passing convention: Option<String> in enum converted to Option<&str> deref at call site
Error-first validation: checks --all, then id, then requires one of them before execution
Consistent error messaging: distinct error for --all unimplemented vs. card ID unimplemented vs. missing both
gotchas_discovered: Implementation is incomplete stub—actual retry logic not yet written
_root parameter unused (prefixed with underscore) suggesting future use or scaffolding for pattern consistency
No distinction made yet between transient error types (rate limits vs. timeouts) in the stub
No validation that card exists in failed/ state before attempting retry
Function signature allows both id and all to be false, which triggers 'must specify' error—this is handled but worth noting for UX
discoveries.approach_outcome: SUCCESS—Added complete command plumbing (enum variant, dispatcher, function signature, parameter validation) without premature implementation; properly scaffolded for future work on actual retry logic
recommendations: Implement card discovery logic to find cards in failed/ with transient error markers in meta.json
Add logic to distinguish transient failures (rate limits 75, temporary network errors) from permanent failures
Consider adding a --dry-run flag to preview which cards would be retried before executing
Move retried cards from failed/ back to pending/ with updated state in meta.json following existing fs::rename pattern
Add tests for --all flag (iterate failed/ directory) and single id case (validate card exists and has transient error)
Document in cmd_retry_transient how transient vs. permanent failures are identified (likely via exit code or error reason in meta.json)
